title: After Kerala, Karnataka becomes Congress’s next leadership challenge
content: The Kerala Congress leadership change has fueled speculation in Karnataka, with Deputy CM D K Shivakumar's supporters pushing for his elevation to the top post. While the party high command has publicly backed Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, Shivakumar's camp sees the Kerala development as a hopeful precedent. The approaching three-year mark of the government's term intensifies the debate over leadership transition.

title: New Keralam govt: VD Satheesan announces cabinet before oath - check full list
content: Kerala chief minister-designate V. D. Satheesan has announced his new cabinet, set to be sworn in on Monday. Prominent leaders like Ramesh Chennithala and Sunny Joseph will be part of the ministry. Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan has been named Speaker, with Shanimol Usman as Deputy Speaker.

title: From July 10, US can deny your visa for this one reason
content: USCIS now has the power to reject or deny immigration applications, even keeping fees, if an invalid signature is found months or years later. This new rule, effective July 10, 2026, applies broadly to most benefit requests, including visas and Green Cards. Applicants cannot usually fix signature errors after submission.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: Indian among dead in Ukrainian drone strike in Russia; Zelenskyy calls attack 'justified'
content: A Ukrainian drone strike in the Moscow region resulted in the death of one Indian worker and injuries to three others. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy stated the attack was justified retaliation for Russia's continued assaults on Ukraine. Russia reported intercepting hundreds of drones in one of the largest offensives of the war.

title: UP man pours acid on sleeping family after woman refuses marriage demand
content: A shocking incident occurred in Shergarh town where a married man allegedly poured acid on a sleeping family of four. The victims, including two young children, suffered severe burns. The accused reportedly targeted the family after the woman repeatedly rejected his marriage demands. Police are actively searching for the suspect and have registered a case.

title: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has a career advice for everyone: Check what it is
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon, at the 2025 Reagan National Economic Forum, urged professionals to "talk to everybody" to foster growth and learning. He emphasized stepping out of comfort zones, observing, and listening to diverse perspectives. Dimon also warned companies against bureaucracy, calling it a "silent killer" that breeds complacency and inefficiency, especially in large organizations.

title: PM Modi’s Netherlands visit: The dam India may look to for water lessons
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to the Afsluitdijk dam in the Netherlands highlighted Dutch expertise in water management, relevant to India's ambitious Kalpasar Project. This mega-project aims to create a freshwater reservoir in Gujarat, addressing water scarcity and flood protection, mirroring the multi-functional Dutch approach to climate resilience.

title: Shreyas Iyer and PBKS script unwanted all-time T20 record after RCB carnage
content: Punjab Kings endured another bowling collapse, conceding 222 to Royal Challengers Bengaluru, setting unwanted T20 records. The team now holds the record for most 200-plus totals conceded in men's T20 cricket history, and captain Shreyas Iyer also set a new captaincy record for such instances. This latest defeat further jeopardizes their playoff hopes.

title: Tamil film producer K Rajan dies by suicide at the age of 85 in Chennai
content: Tamil film producer K Rajan died by suicide on Sunday after allegedly jumping off the Adyar bridge in Chennai, leaving the industry in shock. Rajan, who had a multifaceted career as a producer, writer, director, and actor, was known for his work on films like Brammacharigal and Thunivu. Police have registered a case and are investigating the incident.

title: Watch: How railway staff quickly rescued passengers from burning Rajdhani Express
content: A fire erupted in a Rajdhani Express coach in Ratlam, Madhya Pradesh early Sunday morning. All 68 passengers were safely evacuated from the B-1 coach within 15 minutes. The blaze also damaged the luggage van. The affected coach was detached. Five railway staff were injured in a separate accident during rescue operations. Train traffic on the Mumbai-Delhi route was affected.

title: Bajrang Dal men barge into Dehradun showroom, write ‘jihadi’ on managers’ name tags
content: Bajrang Dal members barged into a Dehradun footwear showroom, alleging Muslim managers harassed Hindu staff and deducted salaries. A video circulating on social media purportedly shows the group confronting Muslim employees and defacing their name tags. Police have not yet registered an FIR, awaiting a formal complaint.

title: Watch: Bangladesh's viral buffaloes resembling Trump, Netanyahu steal spotlight
content: Albino buffaloes resembling Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu are drawing large crowds in Bangladesh ahead of Eid al-Adha. One buffalo, named Trump, is noted for its calm demeanor and golden hair, while another, named Netanyahu, is described as aggressive and mischievous. These unusual animals have become a significant attraction at cattle farms near Dhaka.

title: 'Leader of opposition has right to suggest': BJP ally on Rahul Gandhi's NEET charges
content: The BJP has responded to Rahul Gandhi's demand for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. Party leaders stated that while the opposition has the right to suggest, the government is committed to dismantling the leak network and ensuring accountability. Investigations are ongoing, with arrests made.

title: TVK floor test support sparks row: Dhinakaran warns expelled MLA S Kamaraj of disqualification
content: TTV Dhinakaran announced AMMK MLA S Kamaraj faces disqualification for supporting the ruling TVK government during a floor test. Dhinakaran accused Kamaraj of betrayal and stated the party will pursue disqualification, calling the TVK's acceptance of support 'horse-trading'. He removed Kamaraj from the party, vowing to take necessary steps.

title: 'Eliminated totalling error': Education Ministry defends 'OSM' amid Class 12 result row
content: Following CBSE Class 12 results, Secretary Sanjay Kumar addressed concerns about the pass percentage drop, attributing it to the On-Screen Marking (OSM) system. While OSM, first introduced in 2014, was successfully reimplemented this year, eliminating totalling errors, around 13,000 answer sheets faced legibility issues due to light ink, requiring manual re-evaluation.

title: 'Sarpanch' Shreyas reaches another milestone, joins Dhoni & Kohli to become ...
content: The milestone came during Punjab Kings’ IPL 2026 match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium in Dharamsala.

title: 'Why haven't you sacked education minister?' Rahul slams PM Modi over NEET-UG paper leak
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized Prime Minister Modi over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, questioning the inaction of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Gandhi drew parallels to the 2024 leak, highlighting repeated paper leaks and the government's response. The NEET-UG 2026 exam was cancelled due to paper leaks, affecting 23 lakh aspirants, with the CBI investigating the matter.

title: Why is RCB captain Rajat Patidar not playing vs Punjab Kings? - Explained
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru are without captain Rajat Patidar for their IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings, with Jitesh Sharma leading the side. Patidar is recovering well and is expected to return for the next match in Hyderabad. RCB currently sits atop the IPL 2026 points table.

title: Hyderabad man who slit woman’s throat dies after mob assault
content: A 28-year-old man, Teja, accused of murdering 22-year-old Vaishnavi in Jadcherla by slitting her throat, has died. He was assaulted by local residents after the attack and succumbed to his injuries while undergoing treatment. Police are investigating both the murder and the subsequent mob violence.

title: After Rajdhani Express blaze, relief vehicle rushing to spot flips into gorge in MP
content: A fire on the Rajdhani Express in Ratlam district was averted with all 68 passengers evacuated safely. However, a relief vehicle en route to the scene met with an accident, injuring over five railway employees. The incident disrupted traffic on the Delhi-Mumbai route for hours.

title: 2 Jodhpur sisters die by suicide within 2 months after years of gangrape, blackmail
content: Two sisters have died by suicide in Jodhpur's Kherapa area. The younger sister died Friday after consuming pesticide atop a water tank. She had accused eight people of gangrape and blackmail. Her elder sister died by suicide in March. The family alleges police inaction despite an FIR being lodged. Protests have erupted demanding action against accused and negligent police officials.

title: Ebola virus outbreak: WHO declares international health emergency after rare strain kills 87 in Congo
content: The World Health Organization declared an international health emergency over a rare Bundibugyo Ebola strain in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, with dozens dead. Cases and deaths are rising, particularly in Congo's Ituri province, where community spread and violence complicate containment efforts. This marks Congo's 17th Ebola outbreak.

title: ‘Straight away’: Ravi Shastri backs Sooryavanshi for India debut next month
content: Former India coach Ravi Shastri believes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for a senior India debut, potentially as early as next month on the Ireland tour. Shastri urged selectors to fast-track the Rajasthan Royals opener into the T20 setup, citing his impressive IPL form and fearless batting against experienced bowlers.

title: ‘Recipe for ecological disaster’: Congress writes to Rajnath Singh on Great Nicobar project
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh has urged Defence Minister Rajnath Singh to reconsider the Great Nicobar Island Project, deeming it an "ecological disaster." He argued that while strengthening defence is crucial, the project's commercial aspects outweigh security needs and could harm the region's ecology and tribal rights. Ramesh proposed alternative infrastructure expansions with lower environmental impacts.

title: ‘Tum karlo jo karna hai’: Man caught urinating inside Delhi Metro lift
content: An elderly man urinated in a Delhi Metro lift at Kalkaji station, sparking outrage. Police stated he experienced a medical emergency and couldn't find a toilet, leading to a penalty. The incident highlights concerns about public hygiene and the need for accessible facilities.

title: Nepal’s ‘Everest Man’ breaks his own record, scales Mount Everest for record 32nd time
content: Kami Rita Sherpa has set a new world record by summiting Mount Everest for the 32nd time, while Lhakpa Sherpa achieved her 11th ascent, extending her record for most climbs by a woman. These achievements highlight Nepal's rich mountaineering history amidst a busy climbing season.

title: 'Silence of virgin can be treated as consent': Taliban legalises child marriages with special rules
content: The Taliban regime in Afghanistan has enacted a new family law regulation, "Principles of Separation Between Spouses," which has drawn international criticism. A particularly controversial article interprets a virgin girl's silence after puberty as consent to marriage. The decree also addresses child marriage, divorce, and other marital disputes, granting fathers and grandfathers significant authority.

title: Over 1,000 Telugu expats participate in Mahanadu event in Saudi Arabia
content: Special guests Bode Prasad and TDP leader Mahasena Rajesh interact with the Telugu diaspora and praise efforts to preserve Telugu culture overseas

title: UDF government formation negotiations in Kerala turn into a roller-coaster political ride
content: Media speculation about prospective Ministers and their respective portfolios reaches feverish pitch as television channels air interchanging chyrons about the make-up of the next government on a day when whispers in the corridors of power take the form of news

title: Heavy security, elaborate traffic curbs in place for Kerala Cabinet swearing-in
content: Organisers are anticipating an influx of around 1 lakh people to witness one of the largest political gatherings the State has seen in recent years

title: Kerala Cabinet: Congress leadership in talks with UDF allies to seal allocation of ministerial berths
content: Chief Minister-designate V.D. Satheesan’s office confirmed that the Congress has allocated five Cabinet berths to the IUML, the second-largest constituent of the UDF

title: IUML to get five Cabinet posts in UDF government in Kerala
content: Syed Sadiq Ali Shihab Thangal, IUML State president, identified the Ministers-designate as P.K. Kunhalikutty, P.K. Basheer, V.E. Abdul Gafoor, K.M. Shaji, and N. Samsudheen

title: Over 5,000 metro piers and portals to get advertisements across Bengaluru
content: BMRCL plans to introduce both static and digital advertisement panels on Metro piers and portals, while ensuring that the displays are aesthetically designed and comply with advertisement regulations.

title: Motorists, pedestrians demand that NHAI start work on construction of FOBs in Vellore to prevent accidents
content: Pedestrians, especially women and senior citizens, have to risk themselves in crossing the stretch especially during evening hours as vehicles do not halt for them to cross the carriageway, say residents

title: Kollam fully equipped for monsoon: Collector
content: As part of the anti-waterlogging preparations, intensive cleaning of drainage systems along national highways and other major roads is currently under way

title: Residents demand closure of TASMAC outlets near Girivalam Path, schools in Tiruvannamalai, Vellore
content: The demand for closure of the outlets comes after Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay directed authorities to shut down 717 TASMAC liquor outlets located within 500 metres of places of worship, educational institutions

title: Rythu Sangham demands rollback of fertilizer, fuel price hike
content: The Centre is allowing the companies to shift the burden of rising costs onto the farmers instead of protecting the agricultural sector, it alleges

title: Vijayawada GGH officials suspect conspiracy after dog takes amputated limb
content: There have been internal disputes within the hospital and some outsourcing staff might have conspired in the incident, allege GGH staff

title: 4.02 lakh hectares kharif sowing target set in Yadgir
content: Farmers are waiting for a good spell of showers to carry out intensive agricultural activities, including preparation of land for sowing

title: Kuppam Gangamma Jatara to begin on May 18
content: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu will present silk vastrams to the presiding deity on Wednesday; police and district administration step up security, traffic regulation, and crowd management measures for the event

title: Police personnel injured as protest against demolition drive turns violent in Kolkata
content: People gathered near Park Circus Seven Point Crossing to protest against the demolition drive in Tiljala and attempted to block roads; as police tried to disperse the “unlawful assembly”, a section of the crowd allegedly resorted to stone-pelting, triggering chaos in the area

title: Kishan Reddy urges clean-up of electoral rolls, warns against obstruction
content: The Union Minister said no attempts to obstruct the EC’s work would be tolerated and also cautioned against any effort to intimidate government officials

title: Kerala Cabinet Formation LIVE: UDF finalises Kerala Cabinet list, IUML gets five ministerial posts
content: Senior Congress leader Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan will be Speaker of Kerala Assembly; 21-member Cabinet with CM-designate Satheesan will take oath of office on May 18

title: PM Modi lands in Sweden; to hold talks on bilateral trade, technology, defence | LIVE
content: In his two-day visit to Sweden, Mr. Modi will hold talks with Kristersson, focussing on bilateral relations and explore new avenues of cooperation to enhance bilateral trade

title: Sri Ram Sene stages protest against hijab in educational institutions
content: It terms the Congress government’s move as politically motivated and aimed at appeasing particular communities for vote-bank politics

title: Amalapuram MP takes part in discussions on simultaneous polls
content: Harish Balayogi is part of the JPC that is on a study tour to Bengaluru for extensive interactions on the administrative, institutional and governance dimensions linked to ‘One Nation, One Election’

title: Assam school students learn hands-on water quality testing
content: The skill is becoming necessary in a world with growing pollution concerns, says a biodiversity conservation group that has started the initiative.

title: Indian worker among those killed in Moscow drone strikes, Embassy says
content: Embassy officials ​have visited the ⁠location, met the injured workers ‌in the hospital, and are working with ‌local authorities to ​provide necessary assistance ⁠to the workers, ⁠the statement added.

title: CNG prices hiked second time in 48 hours in Delhi amid increased input cost pressures
content: CNG is now priced from at ₹80.09 per kilogram in the NCT of Delhi with the latest hike. On May 15, 2026 the  prices were hiked by ₹2

title: Mehbooba Mufti backs Hosabale on people-to-people contact with Pakistan
content: “What Hosabale has said now is what (PDP founder) Mufti Mohammad Sayeed advocated till his last breath. Open our roads leading to the other side of Kashmir,” PDP chief Mehbooba Mufti said

title: India’s gems, jewellery exports down 9.07% in April at $2,226.45 million
content: Gold jewellery exports was down 21.77% to $841.54 million in April, compared to $1,075.67 million for the same period last year

title: Plight of betel leaf farmers miserable as gale storms hit fields, expect new government to give relief swiftly
content: The plight of the farmers’ was miserable that most of them are facing ‘red’ and were unable to stop the rising losses due to multiple factors

title: INDIA bloc allies want a strong Congress to take on the BJP at the Centre but unwilling to cede space in States
content: Regional parties within the INDIA bloc expect a strong Congress at the Centre to take on the BJP, but want it to remain a marginal player in the States where they are strong

title: ‘Amma Ariyan’: Restored version of Kerala filmmaker John Abraham’s cult classic to premiere at Cannes
content: The film with a cult following, restored by the not-for-profit organisation Film Heritage Foundation, is the only Indian feature film selected for world premiere at the festival this year. The premiere is scheduled on May 16

title: Fire breaks out in coach of Rajdhani Express, rail-traffic hit
content: The fire broke out in the B-1 coach carrying 68 passengers prompting emergency measures, senior railways commercial manager Sourbah Jain said

title: Suspicion clouds death of pregnant woman in car blaze in Kozhikode
content: Police officers from Meppayur station confirm that all aspects of the case, including forensic evidence, witness statements and family allegations, will be examined in detail as part of the ongoing investigation into the incident

title: Kerala State Electricity Board eyes power procurement to meet peak demand during mid-June to December
content: Power utility is seeking 400 MW for second half of June as well as for August and September, 300 MW in July, and 500 MW each in October, November and December

title: India, Netherlands elevate ties to strategic partnership; ink 17 pacts on defence, critical minerals, other sectors
content: India and the Netherlands inked a number of agreements to further enhance their cooperation in a range of areas

title: West Bengal committed to handover land to BSF within 45 days for border fencing: Minister
content: West Bengal minister Ashok Kirtania said land acquisition in most parts of the North 24 Parganas district was progressing satisfactorily, but flagged resistance in the Basirhat area

title: Sasthra Vedhi calls for green election norms, cites ‘Puthuppally model’ as blueprint for low-carbon poll campaigns
content: Call to establish a maximum ceiling on carbon footprint of candidates contesting in elections, mirroring the existing limits on financial expenditure

title: Vessel carrying 20,000 tonnes of LPG reaches Gujarat after crossing Strait of Hormuz
content: The Marshall Islands-flagged MV SYMI started its journey from Qatar and docked at the port in Kandla around 11.30 p.m. on May 16 after crossing the Strait of Hormuz on May 13

title: Internal deformation detected within ‘rigid’ Indian plate: New study
content: High-precision GPS observations reveal distinct crustal movement patterns within the Indian tectonic plate. Researchers say long-term stresses from the India–Eurasia collision may influence earthquake activity inside the Indian plate

title: Why isn't PM sacking Education Minister Pradhan despite repeated paper leaks, asks Rahul
content: Mr. Gandhi also alleged that the "BJP-RSS nexus" has destroyed India's education system and said the hard work of 22 lakh NEET aspirants has gone to waste

title: Greater Nicobar project a recipe for ecological disaster: Jairam Ramesh
content: The Central government has been citing strategic interests for going ahead, for which the Congress leader has suggested alternative means in a letter the Defence Minister

title: Trump's Advisers Warn China Could Attack Taiwan Within 5 Years: Report
content: Despite the warm atmosphere that surrounded the Trump-Xi summit, those close to the US president are said to be deeply uneasy about Beijing's intentions.

title: Asked To Give Way, 2 Men Stop Bengaluru Woman's Range Rover, Assault Her Husband
content: Rachana alleged in her complaint that the accused were under the influence of alcohol and used vulgar language.

title: 5,300 Chickens Die In Odisha Poultry Farm After Being Trapped In Heat Amid Powercut
content: According to officials and local sources, the total weight of the dead chickens was estimated to be around 12 to 12.5 tonnes.

title: "Shoot Him In Neck Like Charlie Kirk": Chants At UK's Pro-Palestine Rally
content: Protestors at the pro-Palestinian march carried signs that read "smash the far right" and "Free Palestinian Hostages".

title: Karnataka Gang Dressed As Priests "Hypnotises" Shopkeeper, Loots Cash And Gold
content: The police said the accused then handed over two flowers to a shopkeeper, allegedly claiming it would bring prosperity and well-being to him and his children

title: "Show Birth Certificates Of Cows," Says Bengal BJP MLA, Trinamool Reacts
content: The Trinamool wondered if the MLA could show a birth certificate issued to a cow in another state ruled by the BJP.

title: Adani Group Announces Investment And Development Roadmap For Bihar
content: Gautam Adani said the Adani Group plans to invest around Rs 50,000 crore to Rs 60,000 crore in Bihar over the next three to four years

title: Iran-Backed Terrorist Plotted Trump Assassination Through New Proxy Group
content: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi worked with a new Iranian-backed terrorist group that allegedly emerged "overnight" after the United States and Israel attacked Iran.

title: Naveen Patnaik's MP Calls For Bringing  Petro-Products Under GST
content: Under a 28 per cent GST framework, petrol prices could go down by nearly Rs 25 a litre; Diesel prices could reduce by around Rs 14 per litre.

title: Uranium Transfer, Nuclear Limits: US Sets 5 Conditions In Ongoing Iran Talks
content: The US has also reportedly demanded that Iran hand over 400 kilograms of enriched uranium to America as part of the negotiations.

title: New Taliban Law Says 'Silence Of Virgin Girl' Counts As Consent For Marriage
content: The 31-article decree, titled “Principles of Separation Between Spouses", was approved by Taliban supreme leader Hibatullah Akhundzada.

title: Pics: Gautam Adani Performs Bhoomi Pujan For Adani Centre For Eye Diseases In Bihar
content: Adani, accompanied by Priti Adani, chairperson, Adani Foundation, said the Adani Centre for Eye Diseases will be a major effort to provide accessible and affordable eye care to those in need.

title: Karnataka Man Throws Teen Daughter Into Well, Hits With Stones, Buries Her
content: The police said they have evidence to prove that it was her father who killed her after repeated disputes within the family over her marriage.

title: Man Hangs Self After Getting Slapped By Woman In Public In Maharashtra: Cops
content: A man allegedly died by suicide in Maharashtra's Beed district after he was reportedly slapped by a woman in the presence of others, which left him deeply distressed, a police official said on Sunday.

title: Lashkar Terrorist's Mission Detour: A Hair Transplant In Kashmir
content: Infiltrating Jammu and Kashmir to execute terror attacks for the banned Lashkar-e-Taiba (LET), a Pakistani terrorist paused his operations to fulfil a personal dream -- getting a hair transplant.

title: No Doctor, Locked Gate: Faridabad Woman Gives Birth In Hospital Parking Lot
content: The woman delivered a baby boy. Both the mother and newborn were later shifted inside the hospital and are reported to be stable.

title: NP Narvekar, Behind Harvard's $57 Billion Endowment Fund, Plans To Retire
content: Narvekar also serves as a trustee at The Chapin School and is part of the investment committee at Haverford College.

title: In 'Cut Money' Outburst, Exploited Villagers Target Trinamool Leaders
content: Several instances have surfaced across Bengal of the exploited people targeting the houses of Trinamool leaders and demanding the return of 'cut money'.

title: Delhi To Face Severe Heatwave Soon, Temperature May Hit 45 Degrees Celsius
content: Heatwave conditions are expected to prevail till May 22 across Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Chhattisgarh, and parts of Konkan and Goa.

title: 'Jilted Lover' Kills Woman In Telangana, Claims She 'Refused To Return Money'
content: Locals caught hold of the accused before he could flee and thrashed him. Police said the suspect sustained serious injuries in the assault and was shifted to a hospital for treatment under police security.
